question_text: Explain why two kettles that boil water at different speeds have different power ratings, even if they both heat the same amount of water.
correct_answer: The kettle that boils water faster has a higher power rating because it transfers energy more quickly (at a faster rate). Both kettles transfer the same total energy to the water, but the higher power kettle does this in less time.
explanation: Power is the rate of energy transfer. A faster kettle transfers the same amount of energy to the water in a shorter time, which means it has a higher power rating. For example, a 3000 W kettle transfers energy faster than a 1500 W kettle.
